message:
test(ft-b35tw): pattern-pack format spec + JSON Schema + fixture corpus + conformance gate

Closes the conformance gap surfaced by cc_1's audit: the YAML/JSON/TOML
pattern-pack file format had no spec, no JSON Schema, no fixture corpus.
External rule authors had to read patterns.rs to learn the shape; CI
couldn't catch silent drift between the format spec and the loader.

## Files
- docs/patterns-pack-format.md: authoritative format spec. Top-level
  fields (name, version, rules), every Rule field with type + presence
  (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), discovery + sandbox guarantees (cites the
  symlink-escape fix in 6ab52765), versioning policy, reference fixture
  index.
- docs/json-schema/ft-pattern-pack.json: hand-authored JSON Schema
  matching PatternPack (patterns.rs:619) + RuleDef (patterns.rs:487).
  Draft 2020-12, additionalProperties:false on both top-level and
  Rule, snake_case enums for AgentType + Severity. The companion
  validator (jsonschema crate, same workspace dep used by ft-5ikbd's
  envelope schema gate) drives the conformance test.
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/pattern_packs/:
  * valid/minimal_pack.yaml — only required fields
  * valid/full_pack.yaml — every documented Rule field exercised
  * valid/multi_rule_pack.json — multiple rules in JSON form
  * valid/toml_pack.toml — TOML round-trip
  * invalid/duplicate_rule_id.yaml
  * invalid/missing_severity.yaml
  * invalid/empty_name.yaml
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_pattern_pack_format.rs
  (7-test gate):
  * schema_compiles_under_draft_2020_12
  * every_valid_fixture_validates_against_schema (catches schema drift)
  * every_valid_fixture_parses_into_pattern_pack (catches loader/schema
    parity drift)
  * invalid_fixtures_are_rejected_by_the_loader_or_validator (catches
    silent acceptance of malformed packs)
  * every_documented_rule_field_is_exercised_by_some_valid_fixture
    (coverage matrix — new RuleDef field without a fixture update
    flips this red)
  * synthetic_pack_missing_required_field_must_fail (falsification)
  * synthetic_pack_unknown_top_level_property_must_fail (falsification
    of additionalProperties:false)

## Verified
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test conformance_pattern_pack_format
  → 7/7 passed.
- Regression-injection: dropped a fixture with a bogus rule field into
  valid/_regression.yaml; schema gate AND coverage gate both fired red
  (`fixture ... failed schema validation`, `fixtures use rule keys not
  documented`). Restoring the corpus returned all 7 to green.

## Pattern reuse
Mirrors ft-5ikbd (envelope schema validator) for the jsonschema
validator infra, ft-zaqi8 (MCP coverage matrix) for the
matrix-and-meta-test pattern, and the prior golden-corpus beads for
the fixtures+conformance shape. Every documented MUST in the spec maps
to a test in this file; new format fields land via spec+schema+fixture
edits enforced by the gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Two rules with the same `id` — must fail PatternPack::validate().
+name: example.com:dupe
+version: "1.0.0"
+rules:
+  - id: example.dupe
+    agent_type: codex
+    event_type: e1
+    severity: info
+    anchors: ["a1"]
+    description: First copy.
+  - id: example.dupe
+    agent_type: codex
+    event_type: e2
+    severity: info
+    anchors: ["a2"]
+    description: Duplicate id.

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Empty pack name — must fail PatternPack::validate() (and JSON Schema
+# minLength on `name`).
+name: ""
+version: "1.0.0"
+rules:
+  - id: example.empty_name.rule
+    agent_type: codex
+    event_type: e
+    severity: info
+    anchors: ["a"]
+    description: Pack name is the empty string.

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Rule missing the required `severity` field — must fail serde parse.
+name: example.com:bad
+version: "1.0.0"
+rules:
+  - id: example.bad.no_severity
+    agent_type: codex
+    event_type: oops
+    anchors: ["something"]
+    description: This rule omits the severity field.

@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+# Full pattern pack — every documented Rule field exercised at least once.
+# This fixture is also the source-of-truth for the conformance gate's
+# coverage matrix: if a new RuleDef field lands without a fixture
+# update, the gate fires red.
+name: example.com:full
+version: "1.0.0"
+rules:
+  - id: example.full.usage_limit
+    agent_type: codex
+    event_type: usage_limit
+    severity: warning
+    anchors:
+      - "usage limit"
+      - "Please try again"
+    regex: 'usage limit for the (?P<window_hours>\d+)h window'
+    description: Codex hit its rolling-window quota.
+    remediation: Wait for the quota to reset or rotate accounts.
+    workflow: handle_usage_limits
+    manual_fix: "Switch accounts via `caam switch <slug>` and re-run."
+    preview_command: "ft pane peek {pane}"
+    learn_more_url: "https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/rate-limits"
+  - id: example.full.compaction
+    agent_type: claude_code
+    event_type: compaction_required
+    severity: critical
+    anchors:
+      - "Context window full"
+    description: Claude Code signaled context-window exhaustion.
+    remediation: Trigger the compaction workflow and resume.
+    workflow: compact_context

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Smallest legal pattern pack — only required top-level + Rule fields.
+# Exercises: name, version, rules, id, agent_type, event_type, severity,
+# anchors, description.
+name: example.com:minimal
+version: "0.1.0"
+rules:
+  - id: example.minimal.event
+    agent_type: codex
+    event_type: minimal_event
+    severity: info
+    anchors: ["minimal anchor"]
+    description: A minimal rule with only the required fields.

@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+{
+  "name": "example.com:multi-rule-json",
+  "version": "0.2.0",
+  "rules": [
+    {
+      "id": "example.json.gemini_quota",
+      "agent_type": "gemini",
+      "event_type": "quota_exceeded",
+      "severity": "warning",
+      "anchors": ["quota exceeded", "rate limit"],
+      "description": "Gemini quota exceeded.",
+      "remediation": "Wait for reset or rotate API key.",
+      "regex": "quota exceeded.*?(?P<scope>day|minute|hour)"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "example.json.wezterm_pane_died",
+      "agent_type": "wezterm",
+      "event_type": "pane_died",
+      "severity": "info",
+      "anchors": ["[Process completed]"],
+      "description": "WezTerm pane process exited."
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "example.json.unknown_agent",
+      "agent_type": "unknown",
+      "event_type": "generic_error",
+      "severity": "info",
+      "anchors": ["error:"],
+      "description": "Generic error from an unidentified agent."
+    }
+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# TOML round-trip example — same logical content shape as YAML/JSON.
+name = "example.com:toml-pack"
+version = "0.3.0"
+
+[[rules]]
+id = "example.toml.session_start"
+agent_type = "claude_code"
+event_type = "session_start"
+severity = "info"
+anchors = ["[CLAUDE] Session started"]
+description = "Claude Code session began."

@@ -0,0 +1,101 @@
+{
+  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
+  "$id": "https://frankenterm.dev/json-schema/ft-pattern-pack.json",
+  "title": "FrankenTerm Pattern Pack (v1)",
+  "description": "Hand-authored from crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:619 (PatternPack) and :487 (RuleDef). Tracked under ft-b35tw. The companion spec lives at docs/patterns-pack-format.md.",
+  "type": "object",
+  "required": ["name", "version", "rules"],
+  "additionalProperties": false,
+  "properties": {
+    "name": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "minLength": 1,
+      "description": "Pack identifier. Built-in packs use a `builtin:` prefix; user packs use a plain or namespaced name. MUST be non-empty after trimming."
+    },
+    "version": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "minLength": 1,
+      "description": "Pack version. Treated as opaque by the loader; semver-like strings are conventional."
+    },
+    "rules": {
+      "type": "array",
+      "description": "Rules in this pack. Every rule's `id` MUST be unique within the array.",
+      "items": { "$ref": "#/$defs/Rule" }
+    }
+  },
+  "$defs": {
+    "Rule": {
+      "type": "object",
+      "required": [
+        "id",
+        "agent_type",
+        "event_type",
+        "severity",
+        "anchors",
+        "description"
+      ],
+      "additionalProperties": false,
+      "properties": {
+        "id": {
+          "type": "string",
+          "minLength": 1,
+          "description": "Stable rule identifier. SHOULD use a `<agent>.<feature>.<event>` dotted convention."
+        },
+        "agent_type": { "$ref": "#/$defs/AgentType" },
+        "event_type": {
+          "type": "string",
+          "minLength": 1,
+          "description": "Event-type label emitted on match (e.g. `usage_limit`, `compaction_required`)."
+        },
+        "severity": { "$ref": "#/$defs/Severity" },
+        "anchors": {
+          "type": "array",
+          "items": {
+            "type": "string",
+            "minLength": 1
+          },
+          "description": "Literal substrings used by the Aho-Corasick quick-reject pass. SHOULD include at least one anchor."
+        },
+        "regex": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "description": "Optional extraction regex. Named captures (`(?P<name>...)`) are preferred."
+        },
+        "description": {
+          "type": "string",
+          "minLength": 1,
+          "description": "Human-readable description shown in `ft why <rule_id>`."
+        },
+        "remediation": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "description": "Suggested remediation text."
+        },
+        "workflow": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "description": "Suggested workflow name to invoke (e.g. `handle_usage_limits`)."
+        },
+        "manual_fix": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "description": "Manual fix instructions for environments where workflow execution is unavailable."
+        },
+        "preview_command": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "description": "Preview command template supporting `{pane}`, `{event_id}`, `{agent}` interpolation."
+        },
+        "learn_more_url": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "description": "URL for additional documentation about this rule."
+        }
+      }
+    },
+    "AgentType": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "enum": ["codex", "claude_code", "gemini", "wezterm", "unknown"],
+      "description": "Snake-case serialization of frankenterm_core::patterns::AgentType."
+    },
+    "Severity": {
+      "type": "string",
+      "enum": ["info", "warning", "critical"],
+      "description": "Snake-case serialization of frankenterm_core::patterns::Severity."
+    }
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,166 @@
+# Pattern Pack File Format (v1)
+
+> Authoritative spec for the YAML / JSON / TOML files that
+> `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` parses into a
+> `PatternPack`. External rule authors and security auditors should
+> read this document — not the loader source — to learn the format.
+>
+> Tracked under bead **ft-b35tw**. The companion JSON Schema lives at
+> [`docs/json-schema/ft-pattern-pack.json`](json-schema/ft-pattern-pack.json).
+
+## At a glance
+
+A pattern pack is a single document with three top-level fields:
+
+```yaml
+name: builtin:core
+version: "1.0.0"
+rules:
+  - id: codex.usage.reached
+    agent_type: codex
+    event_type: usage_limit
+    severity: warning
+    anchors: ["usage limit", "Please try again"]
+    regex: 'usage limit for the (?P<window_hours>\d+)h window'
+    description: Codex hit its rolling-window quota.
+    remediation: Wait for the quota to reset or rotate accounts.
+```
+
+Discovery rules:
+
+- **Built-in packs** are compiled into the binary and loaded
+  unconditionally.
+- **User packs** under `.ft/patterns/` (any depth) are auto-discovered
+  via `discover_packs_from_dir`. The discovery walker rejects any
+  symlink whose canonical target escapes the workspace root (fix
+  landed at commit `6ab52765`).
+- **Explicit packs** named in `[patterns]` config are loaded from
+  the documented path.
+
+Supported extensions (case-insensitive): `.yaml`, `.yml`, `.json`,
+`.toml`. The extension determines the parser; the on-disk content
+shape is the same across all three.
+
+## Top-level fields
+
+| Field | Type | Presence | Description |
+|-------|------|:--------:|-------------|
+| `name` | string | **MUST** | Pack identifier. Built-in packs use a `builtin:` prefix (`builtin:core`); user packs use a plain or namespaced name (`example.com:custom-rules`). MUST be non-empty after trimming. |
+| `version` | string | **MUST** | Pack version. Convention is semver-like (`"1.0.0"`) but the loader treats it as opaque; MUST be non-empty after trimming. |
+| `rules` | array of [Rule](#rule-fields) | **MUST** | The rule list. MAY be empty for an intentionally-stubbed pack, but every rule's `id` MUST be unique within the pack. |
+
+Unknown top-level fields are tolerated by the parser (default serde
+behavior) but are not part of the spec — authors SHOULD NOT rely on
+them being preserved.
+
+## Rule fields
+
+A rule (Rust: `RuleDef`) has the following fields. `MUST` /
+`SHOULD` / `MAY` follows RFC 2119 semantics.
+
+| Field | Type | Presence | Description |
+|-------|------|:--------:|-------------|
+| `id` | string | **MUST** | Stable rule identifier (e.g. `codex.usage.reached`). MUST be unique within the pack. Authors SHOULD use a `<agent>.<feature>.<event>` dotted convention. |
+| `agent_type` | enum | **MUST** | One of `codex`, `claude_code`, `gemini`, `wezterm`, `unknown`. Serde rename is `snake_case`. |
+| `event_type` | string | **MUST** | The event-type label emitted on match (e.g. `usage_limit`, `compaction_required`). |
+| `severity` | enum | **MUST** | One of `info`, `warning`, `critical`. Serde rename is `snake_case`. |
+| `anchors` | array of string | **MUST** | Literal substrings used by the Aho-Corasick quick-reject pass. SHOULD include at least one anchor; otherwise the regex runs on every input chunk. |
+| `regex` | string | MAY | Optional extraction regex. Named captures (`(?P<name>...)`) are preferred; unnamed groups MAY be used but are not surfaced as `extracted` fields. |
+| `description` | string | **MUST** | Human-readable description shown in `ft why <rule_id>` and the doctor pack listing. |
+| `remediation` | string | MAY | Suggested remediation text. Surfaced in the rendered event template. |
+| `workflow` | string | MAY | Suggested workflow name to invoke (e.g. `handle_usage_limits`). |
+| `manual_fix` | string | MAY | Manual fix instructions for environments where workflow execution is unavailable. Stored as `Option<String>`; absent in the serialized form when null. |
+| `preview_command` | string | MAY | Preview command template supporting `{pane}`, `{event_id}`, `{agent}` interpolation. |
+| `learn_more_url` | string | MAY | URL for additional documentation about this rule. |
+
+Unknown rule fields are tolerated by the parser (silent drop), but
+the conformance gate flags the JSON Schema `additionalProperties`
+contract: pack files SHOULD NOT carry unknown rule fields. CI
+treats this as a warning (not a build failure) so older packs
+continue to load.
+
+## Validation
+
+`PatternPack::validate()` runs in two flavours:
+
+1. **Built-in pack validation** (default for packs loaded via
+   `[patterns]`). Each rule's regex is validated as Rust-regex
+   syntax; anchors are checked for non-emptiness; rule IDs are
+   checked for uniqueness within the pack.
+2. **User-pack validation** (`validate_as_user_pack`). Same rules
+   PLUS additional safety checks: rule IDs MUST start with a
+   namespace prefix (no bare `core.*` or `builtin:*` in user
+   packs); regexes MUST NOT contain unbounded backreferences
+   (`\1`+) that could enable catastrophic-backtracking DoS.
+
+A pack that passes the JSON Schema check MAY still fail validation —
+the schema is a structural gate, not a semantic one.
+
+## Versioning policy
+
+The format itself does not (yet) carry a `format_version` field. The
+file format has been **additive-only since v1**:
+
+- New OPTIONAL rule fields (e.g. `manual_fix`, `preview_command`,
+  `learn_more_url` in recent commits) are backward-compatible.
+- A REQUIRED field rename or removal is a breaking change and MUST
+  be guarded by a `format_version` field bump (deferred until the
+  first such change is needed).
+- Enum widening (new `agent_type` variants) is backward-compatible
+  for readers tolerant of unknown variants. The current loader
+  treats unknown variants as `unknown` (the catch-all variant).
+
+When the first breaking change ships, this spec gains a
+`format_version` row in the top-level table; older packs continue to
+load by treating absent `format_version` as `1`.
+
+## Discovery + sandbox guarantees
+
+- `discover_packs_from_dir` (sandboxed via `sandbox_resolve_dir`)
+  rejects any path whose canonical resolution escapes the workspace
+  root. `..` traversal and symlink-escape are both blocked in a
+  single `fs::canonicalize` call followed by a prefix check
+  (commits `0239fbd6`, `37384b01`, `6ab52765`).
+- Pack files outside the discovery roots are loaded only when
+  named explicitly in `[patterns]` config; the loader does NOT
+  walk `$HOME` or system directories.
+- A malformed YAML/JSON/TOML file fails fast with
+  `PatternError::InvalidRule` carrying the parser error string;
+  the loader does NOT silently ignore unparseable files.
+
+## Reference fixtures
+
+`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/pattern_packs/` ships a
+fixture corpus mirroring this spec:
+
+- `valid/minimal_pack.yaml` — smallest legal pack (one rule, only
+  required fields).
+- `valid/full_pack.yaml` — every documented field exercised at least
+  once.
+- `valid/multi_rule_pack.json` — multiple rules in JSON form.
+- `valid/toml_pack.toml` — TOML round-trip example.
+- `invalid/duplicate_rule_id.yaml` — two rules with the same `id`.
+- `invalid/missing_required.yaml` — rule with no `severity`.
+- `invalid/empty_name.yaml` — pack with `name: ""`.
+
+The conformance test
+(`tests/conformance_pattern_pack_format.rs`) loads each valid
+fixture, validates against the JSON Schema, asserts a successful
+`PatternPack` parse + `validate()`, and confirms each invalid fixture
+fails with the documented error.
+
+## Coverage matrix
+
+The conformance test enumerates every Rule field below and asserts
+each is exercised by at least one valid fixture. New OPTIONAL
+fields landing in `RuleDef` without a fixture update fail the
+matrix check.
+
+## References
+
+- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:619` — `PatternPack`
+- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:487` — `RuleDef`
+- `docs/json-schema/ft-pattern-pack.json` — companion JSON Schema
+- `tests/conformance_pattern_pack_format.rs` — CI gate
+- Sandbox commits: `6ab52765`, `0239fbd6`, `37384b01`
+- Bead: ft-b35tw
